title: Container Packaging, Startup, Health, Migration Coordination, and Rollout · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for container packaging, startup, health, migration coordination, and rollout and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deployable emitted TypeScript feed service. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Inspect packed ESM exports, emitted JavaScript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean Node 24 environment.
Build a pinned non-root minimal image with exact package, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, dependency compatibility, migration state, secrets, budgets, and separate startup, liveness, readiness, degradation, and maintenance semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Ts Progressive Release
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 2706 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind package, non-root container, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
